Microsoft Foundry documentation
The AI app and agent factory - build, optimize, and govern AI apps and agents at scale.

Overview: What is Microsoft Foundry?
Looking for Foundry (classic) portal documentation?

Sections:
- Create apps with code
  - Quickstart - Create Foundry resources
  - Quickstart - Chat with an agent
  - Start with an AI template

- Foundry Models
  - Foundry Models sold directly by Azure
  - Deployment types
  - Get started with DeepSeek-R1
  - Learn how to fine-tune models

- Foundry Agent Service
  - Orchestrate and host AI agents to automate and execute business processes
  - Azure AI app template for agents
  - AI Red Teaming Agent (preview)

- Foundry Control Plane
  - Trustworthy AI
  - What is Content Safety?
  - Trace your application with Microsoft Foundry project library
  - Evaluate agentic workflows
  - Monitor your generative AI applications

- Foundry IQ
  - What is Foundry IQ?
  - Frequently asked questions
  - Connect knowledge base to agents

- Foundry Tools
  - Speech
  - Translator
  - Language
  - Document Intelligence
  - Content Understanding
  - Face

- Azure Machine Learning
  - Learn how to train models
  - Orchestrate workflows with pipelines
  - What is automated machine learning (AutoML)?

- Foundry Local
  - Run LLMs on your device for free
  - Integrate your apps
  - Run HuggingFace models
  - SDK Documentation

SDKs: Python, C#, JavaScript, Java

Further resources:
- Microsoft Foundry portal (ai.azure.com)
- Microsoft Foundry SDKs
- Microsoft Foundry labs
- GitHub Models
- Visual Studio Code extension
